interactions.summary: Jiang explains the church as a religious copy of the Roman household-business system: patriarchs convert, bring their hierarchy with them, and turn obedience into wealth generation.
interactions.speaker_attribution: Jiang lecture with occasional student reading where marked by speaker labels.

speaker_notes.note: Primary speaker is Jiang; non-Jiang segments are mostly student readings or brief student questions rather than independent lecture claims.
speaker_notes.suggested_speaker: Jiang for main exposition; student for marked readings/questions.

claims.claim: Paul copies the Roman paterfamilias model into church hierarchy, replacing patriarch, clients, and slaves with bishop, clergy, and followers.
claims.temporal_scope: Claim stated in the December 2, 2025 lecture about Paul, Christianity, and Roman power.

claims.claim: Christian salvation turns slave labor from survival work into work for eternal paradise, making obedience more productive.
claims.temporal_scope: Claim stated in the December 2, 2025 lecture about Paul, Christianity, and Roman power.

claims.claim: Jiang says Jesus speaks to slaves while Paul speaks to patriarchs, explaining the rapid elite adoption of Christianity.
claims.temporal_scope: Claim stated in the December 2, 2025 lecture about Paul, Christianity, and Roman power.

signature_moments.moment: The business model is brutally simple: a few decades of slave labor are exchanged for eternity in paradise.
signature_moments.source_phrase: work as a slave for a few decades
signature_moments.why_it_matters: This is a memorable Jiang compression that should survive into the public episode read.

chronology_notes.note: Dated 2025-12-02, this packet belongs to Secret History #23 and continues the sequence from Jesus and Paul into the Catholic Church as an enduring power structure.
